So I never feared anything.
I seen bear and everything else, but dog man is a whole different story.
Yes, they are.

After you saw that dogman that night, or as you put it, a huge werewolf, did you ever think about all the time you had spent in the woods already and the likelihood that these guys had been watching you who knows how many times without you knowing it?
Yes, it does change your outlook on everything.
Yes, got a tendency to do that, definitely.
Do you see that as a form of solace, knowing that more than likely you were watched by them countless times, but never harmed, never killed?
You were just observed.
Again, we don't know that's the case, but I bet good money that happened.

I always just had in my mind that they're out there and my friends don't know.
And it's something you can't talk about when you're in the military.
So every time I went to the woods, it was always on my mind, daytime or nighttime.
But I'm still not afraid to go in the woods.
Because it could have killed me that night.
It's good you realize that.
And you mentioned the time you spent in the military.
Which branch did you serve in?
The Army.
Ah, the Army.
Well, thank you so much for your service.
I really appreciate it.
You're welcome.
I do.

you were given an ominous warning when you were about to do an exercise in the Black Forest.

Well, we hide from Special Forces.
They got to find us because of what we have.

but you have no clue what you're watching out for.
I agree that doesn't make sense at all.
Yeah, that's when you're looking for humans.
I'd say so.
As the listeners are going to hear, you've had your fair share of cryptid encounters over the years,
and from what I understand, you had a frightening one last year with something that,
let's just say, wasn't a dog man, and your wife saw it too.
Please tell us the story behind that experience.
Okay, my dog had to go outside.
And she weighs over 100 pounds.
I let her outside.
Her feet hit the grass.
Next thing I know, there's a hundred and some pound dog flying in the air.
And she hit me in the chest about to knock me off my porch.
Her head was looking at the pond.
So I turned my head to the pond to see what she was looking at.
And there's this creature, no hair, long neck, a human face, and long legs staring at me.
Well, I went back inside, and my wife's like, what's wrong?
And I said, I just seen something outside, and it don't make no sense.
Well, the next night, she let the other dog out, and it happened to her.
and I heard her scream and she come flying in the door and she there's something out there I've never
seen before and it's evil and I said well tell me what you saw and she described the same same
thing I just described and she goes I've never seen an animal with a long neck and a human face
I said I don't think that's human babe that's something else so now my head's on a swivel out
here because I do have Bigfoot here.
But they don't bother me.
They stay over there and I stay on my side.
I hear them talk.
I hear them whoop.
I hear everything.
I love sitting outside at nighttime.
That's when all the action starts.
I just don't want to see another dog man.
A dog man and a Bigfoot smell the same, except a Bigfoot don't always smell.
I've been
closest I've been to a Bigfoot is on the side of my glass
window
the closest outside is 10 feet
so they don't
bother me
Dogman does
the government should let people know
these things are real
but I can't imagine how many people have been killed
by these things
I got lucky
well that Dogman did have you dead to rights
as the listeners are going to hear
I mean, like I told you in our first conversation, if it wanted to kill you, it had every opportunity to do that, but it didn't.

What did that dogman smell like?
A little skunk to it, but it's bad BO.

this thing stinks. It stays around.

When I smell that,

My neighbors found out what it smells like, too.
their second day living here.

Big, like black eyes.

They weren like human They were sunken in It put off evil That all I can say You felt the evil on it

I've seen videos. I still don't know what it is.

It was staring right at her, too, she said.

It really is.

Is that because she jumped back towards you on her own or she was thrown?
She jumped back.
I mean, she was that scared.
And she jumped so hard, she jumped high and hit me in the chest.
So she jumped six feet easy.
I knew she was scared.
But when I seen it, I closed the door.
I got her in the door and I closed it.

Oh, no, I believe you.
I believe her too.
Did you see any ears on the side of this thing's head?
No ears.
It was a smooth head.
No hair.
No hair on the body either.

Well, it was dark, so it looked more white.
More like pale white.
The body looked the same.
The body and the head, everything looked the same.
Same color.
That is so freaky.
How tall did you say it was?
It was bigger than a deer.

So it was over four feet, close to five feet on all fours.
I mean, if it would have stood up, it would have been real tall.
You could tell this thing could stand up, but it was on the fours.
I mean, it had kind of like human feet.
It didn't have deer hooves.
I mean, it was just a strange creature.
It sure sounds like it was.

Real long and skinny.
No muscle.
The legs, like I said, were like humans, but it was walking on fours.
There was no bending the knee like how we would have to if we got on all fours.
It was kind of weird.

Yeah, I'd say weird doesn't do it any justice. I can only imagine what you must have been thinking. After what happened that night to your dog, did she all but refuse to go back outside, or were you able to get her to go out without too much trouble?
No, she's always now on the, her head's on a swivel now. She's scared to death that night. Both of my dogs are, so I gotta go out there and stand with them. I put them on chains so they don't run.
because I know Bigfoot will eat them things, so I take care of my animals.
Good man. I'm glad you do that.
All right, Rodney, let's get to brass tacks now.
Please tell us about your encounter.
Give us every last detail that comes to mind.
Okay, I lived outside of Pitchin, Ohio, 1983, August.
It was about 9.30, going on 10, maybe it was dark.
and my car was parked down by the barn.

and when I stepped out and turned around the shrubs,
I kept walking
and next thing I know,

um all i seen was black black eyes but they squinted and he was in a pounced position
and i'm standing two and a half three feet from it i almost walked into it if the light wasn't

the shoulders and goes down the middle of the back down to the back of the rest of the hair is short The tail was a half a tail but wide
The legs were dog legs,
human arms, human shoulders.
The claws were black.
I thought I was going to die.

trying to get away from it.

my feet hit the concrete.
I'm backing up from it.
I take my eye off of it, and it grinned at me.

um i i got back in the house and i'm trying to move a gas stove but you know you can't move them
in front of the door my dad hears what's going on my mom hears what's going on
they jump up and they on what's going on and i'm throwing out this time i can't move the

so

my

Blackwood covered the tracks, walked back out, and told us we did not see anything.

a werewolf.

into human, but it was a

there wasn't no werewolf

Maybe five miles.
Five miles.

They were a problem back then.

not so much so.

she ran into one in 1970-something, 10 years before mine.

Yeah, Germantown ain't that far, really.

No, I won't go hunting in the dark anymore.
Well, I can understand that.
That's pretty normal.

was it standing still when you laid eyes on it, or was it coming to a stop?
Oh, it was already in a pounce position.
It was froze.
It didn't move.
It didn't move its ears.
It didn't do anything until my feet hit that concrete and then it grinned at me

And if you saw its teeth that well, you saw the color of its gums
Did you ever see its tongue?
No, no, did not see it
The teeth were so large you didn't see the tongue
Even the sharp-clicking teeth
I mean, this thing had massive teeth
And like I said, it was meant to rip you apart.

I don't know this for sure, but when we spoke about your encounter for the first time, it seemed like it was a toss-up between it smiling at you and when it was crouching, acting like it was getting ready to pounce on you.
I wasn't really sure which one of those actions shook you up most, but listening to you tonight, it sounds like the smile shook you up the most.
Is that about right?
Yes, it did. When it smiled, that put a fear in me. I never felt. I've never felt that way ever again. That put a fear in me I never want.
When I come around that corner and I was standing right there in his face, I'm guessing 10 seconds. I didn't move. I was frozen.
I had to get my mind back and I was like, God, let me get out of this.
And so I started backing up and I forgot to tell you, I was backing up and I was talking to it at the same time.
And I was telling it easy easy And when my feet hit that concrete dude that grin that just showed me intelligence I never seen in an animal
I can't think for these things.
No one can, but I bet you good money it was grinning because it was loving every minute of that.
It could tell it really shaking you up, and it was just soaking it up.
Oh, yeah.
That's exactly how I felt.
I'll bet you that's how it was
I couldn't tell nobody
I couldn't tell my friends, I couldn't tell nobody
We were threatened over this
And plus how are you going to tell people you've seen a werewolf?
That's a good point
Yeah, it's really unfortunate
It's bad enough to have an encounter like that
But when you're dealing with the government
Putting the kibosh on you being able to reach out to people for help
That just makes it that much more difficult
It really is a shame

No, I said, God, get me out of this.
I said it out loud.
And when me talking at first, it didn't jump.
It was just listening to what I was saying and didn't react until my feet hit that concrete.
And then it's like, I could add you.
So just to get some clarity, when you asked God to help you get out of there, it didn't
respond to God's name?
I think it did.
That's why it grinned.
I mean, it's always stuck with me about that.
Did it grin at me because I mentioned God or did it grin at me because I had you?
And or I like messing with you.
So no signs of discomfort from it when you mentioned God's name then?
No, no, I just felt the, I felt it hated me. It hated us, humans. That's what I got.
Well, you might be right. A lot of eyewitnesses have reported that very thing. So, yeah, there just might be something to that.
As you started backing up, trying to put some distance between you and it, telling it easy, easy, did you hold your hands up or keep them down at your sides?
I had him up. I was like, you know, waving to him real slow and just saying, easy, big guy, easy.
And I just kept backing up. I do the same thing with Bigfoot.
You know, when I get too close, you know, snap a tree or something, grab me.
I'll say, easy, big guy. And I was back up. It's just the way I am.
That was an awfully difficult situation there, Rodney, but you sure did handle that as well as it could be handled.
I'm really impressed.
I really am.
When you don't know what you're looking at, you just don't know how to react to that.

What went through their minds.
Yeah, it does get pretty bad.
You told us what it smelled like.
Here's something you might not have considered.
How sure are you, considering you're face-to-face with the thing?
It's so tall on four legs, it's literally nose-to-nose with you.
Again, even though it's on all fours, how sure are you that that stench you smelled wasn't just its breath, that it wasn't its body odor, in other words, or vice versa?

Like I said, the only time it opened his mouth was when it grinned.

It didn't smell like a skunk.
And I kept thinking, what is that smell?

The next night when he came back, we smelled it first.
And then I watched it climb up the tree.

Dogs don't climb trees.
And it was looking in my window.
That thing was smart.

You see a werewolf looking in your window, and I'm on the second story.

That would have really messed you up.
Understandably so, too.
How close was that tree to your window?
Um, 30 feet.
30 feet, about 30 feet.
Thank goodness it wasn right on the window where it could have reached out and tapped on the glass or anything Oh yeah It launched itself off that tree
It just sprung off the tree and landed in the cornfield.
And what got me is that the distance it landed and the height, it didn't even hurt it.
It's like he's done this before.
Oh, I'm sure they do that all the time.
When the game warden came out and gave you that warning not to talk about your encounter,
do you think he might have done that to protect you, or are you pretty sure that he did it for another reason?

Drop it, because they will make our life miserable.

but it sounds like you did know him, or the family knew him,

So, I mean, if you're out hunting, you see old Blackwood somewhere.

We knew him.
But, no, he just kept saying, don't make your life miserable.
So, yeah, he was black.
Protecting us, I say.
It just made me mad.
It's like I acknowledge these things alive.
I can understand why it would make you mad.
That's totally understandable.
You lived in the woods when you had that encounter.
Do you still live in the woods now, or have you sworn off ever doing that again?
No, I live out in the country.
I got woods all around me.
They've done Bigfoot research out here.
I don't want to see a dogman out of here.
But they have a spring, ponds.
I've got a five-acre pond in front of me, a pond beside me, and in the spring it runs out back.
The deer disappeared for two years around here.
And this year is the first time we've seen deer again.
They usually run the deer out of here.
You did tell us that you have Sasquatch around your property, but you can have Sasquatch around without living in the woods.
I mean, if you live out in farmland, you can have them around and they just move in from the forest.
